extraction.one_line_summary: DE GENDT Thomas attacked MOHORIČ on the final climb ascent with 3km to go, dropped him on descent to win stage 7.
tactical_patterns: MOHORIČ waited for DE GENDT after every descent drop instead of forcing selection, enabling final-climb counterattack vulnerability.
Movistar rode defensively with MAS pacing climbs despite VALVERDE needing bonus seconds, letting 5-minute break succeed with own riders.
BOUWMAN chased alone from breakaway remnants, reaching 1-minute deficit, preventing MOHORIČ from sitting on DE GENDT earlier.
Ineos used CARAPAZ as GC policeman after TT losses eliminated his own GC threat, critical domestique conversion pattern.

rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: fifth Volta Catalunya stage win, decisive attack final climb with 3km remaining exploited MOHORIČ descent waiting tactics
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: breakaway every stage showing Ardennes classics form but tactically passive waiting after descent gaps cost stage win
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: first European WorldTour GC win, only 7-second TT loss to ALMEIDA massive improvement from 30-second UAE deficit
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: stage 3 climbing level impressive at 41, should have been Movistar sole GC focus not shared leadership
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: last-kilometer surges showing 2018 climbing level return, critical GC depth signal for Tour preparation
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Giro champion relegated to domestique policing breaks after TT deficit, ate into MAS/CHAVES gaps on stage 4
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: gained 3.5 minutes stage 4 while close on GC until Ineos domestiques ROWE/CASTROVIEJO/DENNIS shut down
rider_signals.BOUWMAN Koen: positive: solo chase from breakaway remnants reached 1-minute gap, forced MOHORIČ to work against DE GENDT
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: paced all climbs for Movistar but team tactics prioritized defensive GC over VALVERDE stage/bonus offensive
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: only 6-second TT loss to ALMEIDA signals sustained GC level, second overall confirms Tour co-leadership viability
